display.purpose: Montserrat — marketing/landing headlines and the largest page titles only.

title.purpose: Plus Jakarta Sans 600 — card titles, section headers, modal headings.

body.purpose: Plus Jakarta Sans 400 at 14px — default UI text, table cells, form values.

label.purpose: Plus Jakarta Sans 500 at 12px, muted — field labels, column heads, chip text.

mono.purpose: Geist Mono — admission numbers, codes, aligned numeric columns.

body-tabular.purpose: Money and numeric cells — fees, balances, marks, counts, percentages. Body size/weight with tabular figures (tnum) so digits align in columns and totals scan.

shadows.name: accent-lift
shadows.purpose: Primary button only — a faint indigo lift on the one action that matters.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Modals, dropdowns, popovers — surfaces truly floating above the page.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Default state transitions (hover/press) on interactive surfaces.

motion.name: duration-slow
motion.purpose: Larger reveals and lifts; upper bound for calm motion.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Entrance easing — fade-and-rise; no bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: hover-lift
motion.purpose: Subtle lift on interactive surfaces (cards, frames) on hover.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single high-emphasis action on a view. Solid indigo with a faint accent lift.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; height: 36px; padding: 0 14px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #fff; background: #4f46e5; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 6px rgba(99,102,241,0.35); cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 150ms; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #4338ca; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #3730a3; transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.5); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Standard secondary action. White, hairline border, slate text.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; height: 36px; padding: 0 14px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #334155; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms, border-color 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #f8fafc; border-color: #cbd5e1; color: #0f172a; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.5); }

components.name: Ghost Icon Button
components.description: Low-emphasis action for dense rows; gains a slate wash on hover. Shown with an inline edit icon.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-ghost" aria-label="Edit"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M12 20h9"/><path d="M16.5 3.5a2.12 2.12 0 0 1 3 3L7 19l-4 1 1-4Z"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; color: #94a3b8; background: transparent; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #f1f5f9; color: #4f46e5; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.5); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard field. White, hairline border, generous rounding, calm indigo focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 44px; width: 240px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #0f172a; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 12px; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms, box-shadow 150ms; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: #cbd5e1; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #818cf8; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.15); }

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: The base container. Flat white plane defined by a hairline border, generous 16px corners.
components.html:
Class 1A — Attendance
32 students · 28 present today
components.css: .ds-card { width: 280px; background: #fff; border: 1px solid rgba(226,232,240,0.8); border-radius: 16px; overflow: hidden; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; } .ds-card-head { padding: 20px 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid #f1f5f9; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; color: #0f172a; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-card-body { padding: 20px 24px; font-size: 14px; color: #111827; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Status pill — soft tinted background, saturated same-hue text, always with a word. Shown in success state.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 999px; } .ds-chip-success { background: #dcfce7; color: #15803d; }

narrative.overview: Skula is the instrument a school trusts with its records and its money, so it behaves like a good ledger: precise, calm, and quiet. Chrome recedes; the data is the loudest thing on screen, and even it stays orderly. White surfaces on a soft gray canvas, a single indigo accent for pointing, flat planes separated by hairline borders. The more information a screen carries, the quieter everything around it becomes. It rejects the dense gray PHP admin it replaces, generic Bootstrap templates, over-designed dashboards, and childish school clip-art.
keyCharacteristics: Calm, low-contrast chrome around high-contrast data
One indigo accent, used sparingly, for the primary action and active state
Flat surfaces separated by hairline borders, not shadows
Generous corner rounding (12-16px) for a soft, modern, non-corporate feel
Status is always color plus a label/icon, never color alone
Dense-but-orderly: tables, filters, tabs, and chips behave identically everywhere

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Indigo is the only brand color and appears on ≤10% of any screen — the primary action, the active state, a link. If two indigo things compete for attention, one is wrong.

rules.name: The Status-Never-Alone Rule
rules.body: A status color is always paired with a word or icon (PAID, Present, ✓ Complete). Color is reinforcement, never the sole signal.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Montserrat speaks only for display/marketing and the biggest titles; everything functional is Plus Jakarta Sans.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest, defined by a 1px slate-200 border. A shadow is earned only by the single primary action or a true overlay.

rules.name: The Tabular-Figures Rule
rules.body: Every cell that renders money, a balance, a mark, or a count uses tabular figures (font-feature-settings: "tnum"). Proportional digits make columns ragged and totals hard to compare; tabular digits are the quiet signature of a system you trust with numbers. Never render a money cell without it. (Adapted from Stripe.)

rules.name: The Eyebrow Exception
rules.body: Titles and body run tight or neutral tracking, but small uppercase eyebrows/section kickers take positive tracking (~0.14em) — the one place letter-spacing opens up, to mark taxonomy rather than emphasis. (Adapted from Linear.)

rules.name: The Dark Anchor Rule
rules.body: The left sidebar is the app's one deliberately dark region (slate-900): muted uppercase section labels, slate-300 items, white on hover, and a solid Indigo Signal pill on the active item — licensed only by the dark ground. On light surfaces the active state stays text/indicator; the pill counts toward the ≤10% indigo budget, so adjacent chrome (banners, badges) stays muted.

dos: Do reserve Indigo Signal (#4f46e5) for the primary action, active state, and links (≤10% of any screen).
Do define surfaces with a 1px slate-200 border on the gray-50 canvas; keep them flat.
Do pair every status color with a word or icon.
Do keep Montserrat for display/titles and Plus Jakarta Sans for everything functional.
Do run app text at 13-14px with comfortable line-height.
Do give destructive actions a confirm step and a soft (not solid-red) treatment.
Do render every money/mark/count cell with tabular figures (tnum) so columns align and totals scan — the Tabular-Figures Rule.
Do keep motion functional and quick (150–300ms, ease-out) and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't recreate the dense, gray, dated PHP admin look — feature parity, never UI parity.
Don't ship a generic Bootstrap admin template appearance with no point of view.
Don't over-design: no decorative gradients, no attention-seeking motion, no heavy resting shadows.
Don't use childish, cartoonish, primary-color school clip-art.
Don't let indigo exceed ~10% of a screen or introduce a second competing accent.
Don't convey status with color alone.
Don't let Montserrat leak into buttons, inputs, or table cells.
Don't put semantic status colors (success green, danger red) on solid button fills — keep them as text + soft-tinted chips; a solid green/red block reads as a trading terminal, not a ledger.
Don't render money or numeric columns with proportional figures (missing tnum) — ragged digits break the financial-data signature.
